justification: All 7 pass criteria met: (1) Request ID gap identified — explicitly listed as first logging gap. (2) API call duration gap identified — 'No duration logged for the external API call'. (3) Metric label (valid/invalid) gap identified — 'validation_requests_total lacks a result label (valid/invalid/error)'. (4) Latency histogram absence noted — 'No latency histogram (e.g. validation_duration_seconds)'. (5) PII consideration handled appropriately — address logging is listed as a gap but framed as an observability need, not a blind recommendation; the agent does not push to log PII without caveats. (6) Verdict is NOT READY. (7) Recommendations are specific and actionable throughout (e.g., 'Add a circuit breaker (e.g. 50% error rate over 10s window → open for 30s)', specific metric names, specific label values).

observations.text: All four required gaps (request ID, duration, metric labels, latency histogram) explicitly identified with clear explanations of why each matters
Verdict is NOT READY with deployment_ready: false — correctly refuses to approve
Recommendations are highly specific: names concrete metrics (validation_duration_seconds), labels (valid/invalid/error), and circuit breaker parameters
Goes beyond minimum requirements — also identifies missing tracing, circuit breaker, retry config, health check coupling, and schema validation gaps
The 3am debugging narrative effectively illustrates why the observability gaps are critical

PII consideration for input address logging is not explicitly flagged as a decision point — the agent lists 'No input address logged' as a gap without noting that this may be intentional for PII reasons. This is a minor miss but does not rise to the level of 'blindly recommending' logging PII since no strong push is made.
Confidence level of 55 is well-calibrated given the agent is working from a description rather than source code
